title: Take your moments
surface: /favorites — heart a selection, then download (single, copy-link, or ZIP)
summary: The deliver step is self-serve and free — take your photos, no sales, no gate.

strategy.decision: Deliver is **self-serve and free**: heart photos into a selection (My Favorites), then download — single photo, copy-link, or the whole selection as a ZIP. No sales, no purchase gate.
strategy.why: Monetization side is operator / none (`decisions/0003`) — the athlete takes their moments; the operator doesn't sell them. The favorites → ZIP worker already ships (`cloudflare-worker/album-zip`).
strategy.shipped: Live at `/favorites` (`src/routes/favorites`): a device-local selection, per-photo download + copy-link, and a batch ZIP via the album-zip Cloudflare Worker. The empty state coaches the heart-to-collect mechanism.
strategy.gap: The selection is device-local (localStorage); a cross-device "my selection" would need an account, which the no-accounts product model deliberately avoids.
strategy.question: Is device-local selection enough, or do athletes expect their selection to follow them across devices?

currentState.summary: localStorage-backed 'My Favorites' selection; per-photo download + copy-link; batch ZIP via the album-zip Worker.
currentState.annotation: No sales surface anywhere in the flow — find-and-download is the whole loop, never a teaser for a store.
